diffCoverage.notes: Root cause confirmed from the upstream model: zca-rust TMessage carries d_name but no avatar, so save_message could only ever store thread_avatar=None. Fix backfills identity from the directory the app already fetches. lib.rs + session.svelte.ts appear in git status but are prior-task changes this task did not need to touch.

findings.evidence: backfill_thread_identities is a bare UPDATE (no INSERT) scoped by (account_id, thread_id), so a directory entry with no conversation does not create a row; COALESCE plus the empty-string filter on title/avatar means a missing/empty directory value never blanks a known one. rusqlite stays confined to the store layer; ThreadIdentity is a pure types DTO; command/ reaches the store only via the injected StoreState and returns non-secret DTOs. The offline test thread_identity_backfill_updates_existing_only asserts updated==2 (the two existing threads) and that u404 creates no row; the ignored thread_identity_cache_roundtrip proves the title+avatar survive a close+reopen with no realtime event or directory fetch. tests/lint/build/structural/smoke checks are attested with exitCode 0.
findings.fix: None required to mark done. Persisting the full directory as its own table is recorded as an accepted low risk and an explicit non-goal.

summary: fix-thread-avatar-persistence is honestly done. The user's observation was correct: chat history did not persist conversation avatars (and group titles were the last sender) because the realtime message model has no avatar field, so save_message always wrote thread_avatar=None. The fix backfills existing thread rows with title+avatar from the contacts/groups directory the app already fetches, in the store layer, called from the command layer after the directory load. Forward-only layering [types -> store -> command] intact, no secrets cross IPC, UPDATE-only so no phantom threads. Offline test + restart smoke prove durability; build/lint/tests/structural clean. Pass.
